method_description: Standalone opt-out page at /optout asks only for an email address. User enters email, completes reCAPTCHA v2, clicks 'Send verification link'. A verification email is sent and the user must click the link to complete the removal. No name, city, or state search is available. No record selection step — the broker identifies the record internally by email only. There is no public people search; all search features redirect to login/registration.
bot_check: reCAPTCHA v2 on the opt-out page. Confirmed: data-sitekey present, google.com/recaptcha/api.js loaded, standard reCAPTCHA v2 checkbox widget rendered.
notes: ContactOut is a B2B professional contact data platform (email finder for recruiters and salespeople). Opt-out URL found at /optout, confirmed by the privacy_data_subjects page which states: 'you may opt out here: https://www.contactout.com/optout'. The opt-out form is email-only — no name+city+state search is available. All search features (homepage search, /people-search, /search-portal, /search) redirect to /register or /login, requiring account creation. Tier 1 fails on: (1) Search not supported — no public search by name + city + state. (2) Blind opt-out — user cannot search for or select their record; broker identifies records internally by email only. Email verification link is part of the flow but is a Tier 2 concern (PIR supports email confirmation per the rubric). The form uses reCAPTCHA v2 (supported type). California check not applicable as the opt-out form has no state selection field — same single-field email form for all users. B2B scope concern: ContactOut serves professional/business contact data, not traditional consumer people-search data. Footer contains 'Do Not Sell My Info' text but no clickable link was found for it. Privacy policy page links to /privacy_data_subjects for data subject rights. Privacy policy also states users can opt out by emailing support@contactout.com.
